chunks.en: I usually take a short break in the middle of the afternoon.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい午後の真ん中あたりに短い休憩を取ります。

chunks.en: When I feel tired, I stand up and stretch my arms and back.
chunks.ja: 疲れを感じたら、立ち上がって腕や背中を伸ばします。

chunks.en: What I like to do is get a hot cup of coffee.
chunks.ja: 私が好きなのは、温かいコーヒーを一杯もらってくることです。

chunks.en: Where I usually go is the break room next to my desk.
chunks.ja: 私がいつも行くのは、机のとなりにある休憩室です。

chunks.en: Sometimes I go outside for a few minutes to get fresh air.
chunks.ja: 時々、新鮮な空気を吸うために数分だけ外に出ます。

chunks.en: After a short break, I feel calm and I can focus much better.
chunks.ja: 短い休憩のあとは、気持ちが落ち着いて、ずっと集中できるようになります。

outline: Opening: when I take a short break
Point 1: how I relax with stretching and a drink
Point 2: where I go, like the break room or outside
Wrap-up: how I feel after the break

modelTalk.text: I take short breaks at work every day. I usually take my first break in the morning, around ten. Then I take another one after lunch. I don't wait too long. When I feel tired, I stop and rest. I like to stand up and stretch my arms. Sometimes I get a glass of water or some tea. That helps me feel better fast. I often walk to the break room and sit down. On nice days, I go outside for fresh air. I don't use my phone much then. I just breathe and look around. After a short break, I feel calm and happy. My head feels clear again, and I'm ready to work. It's a small thing, but it really helps me. So I try to take breaks every day. That's how I stay relaxed.

chunks.en: I usually take a short break in the early afternoon, around two o'clock.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい午後の早い時間、2時ごろに短い休憩を取ります。

chunks.en: What I do to relax is stretch my arms and get a cup of coffee.
chunks.ja: リラックスするためにやるのは、腕を伸ばしてコーヒーを一杯もらうことです。

chunks.en: Where I usually go is the break room, or sometimes outside for fresh air.
chunks.ja: 私がよく行くのは休憩室で、たまには新鮮な空気を吸いに外へ行きます。

chunks.en: After a short break, I feel calmer and much more ready to focus again.
chunks.ja: 短い休憩のあと、私は気持ちが落ち着いて、また集中する準備がぐっと整った気分になります。

chunks.en: So even a five-minute break really helps me stay productive through the day.
chunks.ja: だから5分の休憩でも、一日を通して生産的でいるのに本当に役立ちます。

outline: Opening: when I take a short break
Point 1: how I relax, like stretching and coffee
Point 2: where I go, break room or outside
Wrap-up: how I feel and why breaks help

modelTalk.text: I take my first short break in the middle of the morning, around ten. By then I've been staring at my screen for hours, and my eyes feel tired. So I stand up and stretch my arms and back for a minute. It really helps me loosen up. Then I usually walk to the kitchen and get a drink, like water or coffee. Sometimes I go outside for a bit of fresh air, especially when the weather's nice. I don't check my phone, because I want my brain to rest too. Other times I just chat with a coworker in the break room. It's a nice way to switch off for a while. After a short break, I feel much calmer and more awake. I can focus again, and the rest of my work doesn't feel so heavy.
